Our lab follows the [Google R Style Guide](https://google.github.io/styleguide/Rguide.html),
which in turn mostly follows the [tidyverse style guide](https://style.tidyverse.org/) [@wickham2023tidyverse].
The following principles apply to all R code:

- **Follow tidyverse conventions**: Our style is built on the tidyverse style guide,
  which provides comprehensive guidance on naming,
  syntax,
  pipes,
  functions,
  and more
- **Naming**: Use `snake_case` for functions and variables;
  acronyms may be uppercase (e.g., `prep_IDs_data`)
- **Write tidy code**: Keep code clean, readable, and well-organized
- **Avoid redundant logical comparisons**: Use logical variables directly in conditional statements (e.g., `if (x)` instead of `if (x == TRUE)` or `if (x == 1)`)

{{< include coding-style/_pipes.qmd >}}

Many of these rules are automatically enforced through our [`.lintr.R`](https://github.com/UCD-SERG/lab-manual/blob/main/.lintr.R) configuration file.
See @sec-style-auto-style for details on automated style checking.
